steps.title: Identify the linear function form
steps.content:
Interpret the equation $$ f(x)=6x+5 $$ as slope-intercept form $$ y=mx+b $$.
Here the slope $ m=6 $ means the tree grows 6 feet each year, and the y-intercept $ b=5 $ gives the height when $ x=0 $, i.e., at the first measurement.

steps.title: Evaluate at x=0 to find initial height
steps.content:
Substitute $ x=0 $ into the function:
$$ f(0)=6\cdot0+5=5 $$
Thus the estimated height at the time of first measurement is 5 feet.
That matches the correct answer; D is correct.
